evidence.scientific_clarity: Table clearly shows 4 methods x 5 metrics with bold best-value logic and italic group labels (Baselines / Ours); OursModel dominance is immediately legible.
evidence.typography: pdfTeX-generated with Computer Modern Type 1 fonts (CMR10, CMBX10, CMTI10) all embedded; no DejaVu Sans; 10pt body size appropriate for camera-ready.
evidence.palette: Pure black-and-white table; no color used, so colorblind safety is trivially satisfied and no directional/categorical color discipline issues arise.
evidence.layout_density: Booktabs rules (toprule/midrule/bottomrule), no vertical lines, compact subscript std notation, grouped rows with italic labels; no overlapping text observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly states bold convention and 5-seed count as required, but neither caption nor table highlights a specific numerical finding to orient the reader.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdfTeX 1.40.29 produces genuine vector PDF 1.7; pdftotext extracts all cell values and caption cleanly; all 8 Type 1 font subsets embedded.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; table.tex is explicitly required by the brief but absent from the output directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Explains bold convention and seed count (both brief requirements) but contains no specific numerical claim or highlighted finding (e.g., OursModel gains on GPQA).

overall_strengths: Genuine vector PDF with fully embedded Computer Modern fonts and selectable text — no rasterisation artifacts, passes pdffonts check cleanly.
Correct booktabs structure with group-spanning italic labels, subscript std formatting, and bold logic that handles within-1-std ties as specified.
overall_weaknesses: table.tex is missing from the output directory despite being an explicitly required deliverable in the brief — a reviewer or downstream user cannot recompile or modify the LaTeX source.

Caption reads as a method description rather than a result statement; adding one sentence with a concrete numerical takeaway (e.g., 'OursModel leads on all five metrics, with the largest margin on GPQA (+5.2 pp over the best baseline)') would substantially improve reviewer readiness.
